steps.title: Identify the right triangle configuration
steps.content: Triangle QRS has a right angle at R (marked by the small square). The base SR has length 22. Side QS is the hypotenuse we're looking for.

steps.title: Apply the sine ratio from angle Q
steps.content:
From angle Q, side SR is opposite and QS is the hypotenuse. Using the sine ratio:
$$\sin Q = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \frac{22}{QS}$$
Rearranging to solve for QS:
$$QS = \frac{22}{\sin Q}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
